pi_items.source: Web fetch and HTTP verification. Confirmed via curl HEAD and GET requests. Site is a real, publicly accessible demo website for web scraping.

Web content inspection. Homepage and category pages contain multiple links with /catalogue/ path segments, e.g. 'catalogue/category/books/travel_2/index.html', 'catalogue/category/books/mystery_3/index.html'. Confirmed via curl request to homepage.
This is an arbitrary replacement value for the simulation. Not verifiable against external sources.

Web content inspection shows the homepage alone contains at least 5+ visible catalogue URLs (category links). The site has 50 pages of products plus multiple category pages. Minimum threshold of 10 is easily achievable and reasonable.

Analysis of setup_task.sh and export_result.sh. The export directory /home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports is created in setup (line 28-29), and export_result.sh checks for the file at this exact path (line 15). Path follows standard Linux user home directory structure.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses books.toscrape.com, a real, publicly accessible demo website designed for web scraping practice. The site contains abundant URLs with the /catalogue/ path segment (e.g., catalogue/category/books/travel_2/index.html, catalogue/category/books/mystery_3/index.html) that would be discovered during a crawl. The minimum rewritten URL count of 10 is reasonable given the site's structure with 50+ pages and multiple category/product links. The export path /home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports/migration_simulation.csv follows standard Linux user directory conventions and is properly created and checked by the task scripts.
